channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight Switch 2 Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
TT Games has revealed that LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will release for the Switch 2 in September 2026.

The latest LEGO adventure from TT Games was released earlier this month and is receiving positive reviews from both critics and players. As of now, the game boasts a Metascore of 84 and a user score of 8.7. Now, the developers have confirmed that the title will soon be available on a brand new platform.

LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight Releases for Switch 2 on September, Pre-Orders Are Live

A new announcement on the official X account (formerly Twitter) of LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight reveals that the Switch 2 release date is September 18, 2026. Interested readers can purchase the game now on the Nintendo Store for $69.99. Players who pre-order the game will receive the “The Dark Knight Returns” batsuit.

About LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight

In LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, players will be able to go through the origins of the cape crusaders, starting from his days with the League of Shadows, to becoming the savior of Gotham City. Players will also meet various characters they can play as, including Jim Gordon, Catwoman, Robin, Nightwing, and Batgirl. There will be a variety of DC Super Villains to face off against, including The Joker, The Penguin, Mr. Freeze, Poison Ivy, Bane, and more.

The new game also features a fresh combat system that lets players leverage combos, stealth, and detective skills. You will have access to different types of Batmobiles and Batcycles to get across the city, and there are other traversal options, such as grappling and gliding, too. The map features different types of crimes, challenges, rewards, secrets, and iconic landmarks such as Arkham Asylum, Ace Chemicals, and Wayne Tower.

In other news, the first information on LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight DLC appears to have been datamined, revealing several upcoming names. Additionally, here are all the details on the voice actors behind your favorite characters in LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight.

What are your thoughts on LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight releasing for the Switch 2 this September? Will you be buying it? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a claim stating that a general Nintendo Direct is planned for next week. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: NASCAR Says Sim Racing Is Becoming the New Path Into Motorsports
item.content:encoded:
For decades, aspiring race car drivers followed a familiar path. Start in karting, work through local tracks, attract sponsors, and hope the opportunities eventually lead somewhere bigger.

According to NASCAR Senior Director of Sim Racing Ray Smith, that path is changing.

While traditional grassroots racing remains important, Smith believes simulator racing has become one of the most effective ways for aspiring drivers and motorsports professionals to break into the industry.

“You want to write music, you go to Nashville. You want to be a model, you go to New York. You want to be an actor, you go to L.A. You want to be in motorsports, you go to Indy or Charlotte,” Smith told Insider Gaming. “But nowadays, you want to be in motorsports, go to the sim, go to iRacing.”

It is a significant statement from one of the people overseeing NASCAR’s esports efforts, particularly as sim racing continues to grow following its explosion in popularity during the COVID-19 pandemic.

  • More Sim Racing: The Best Things I Saw At SimRacing Expo Charlotte 2026

While drivers such as William Byron and Rajah Caruth have become some of the most recognizable examples of competitors who used sim racing as a stepping stone toward real-world opportunities, Smith said the impact extends far beyond those headline stories.

“Four out of five of our last champions are now sim engineers in a Cup shop,” Smith said. “We have content creators coming out of our series.”

The opportunities created through sim racing are no longer limited to getting behind the wheel. Smith pointed to careers in engineering, media, content creation, and team operations that have emerged through connections built within the online racing community.

“We have guys that are journalists, content creators, engineers,” Smith said. “You can see it on our storyboard over there.”

Smith noted that Caruth’s rise through sim racing has become one of the sport’s most visible success stories. After seeing Byron’s path from iRacing to NASCAR, Caruth used a similar blueprint to launch his own racing career.

“William Byron, obviously, the biggest story, because that’s where Rajah got his playbook from,” Smith said. “He saw William do it. He’s like, ‘Oh, okay, that’s the roadmap.'”

For NASCAR, those examples are becoming an important recruiting tool as the organization works to attract younger audiences and future talent.

“I think that story, we’re working on telling that story more and more and more because we want that young Raja Pruth kid to see that roadmap,” Smith said.

The accessibility of sim racing is a major part of that appeal. Unlike traditional racing, which often requires significant travel, equipment, and financial investment from a young age, sim racing offers an opportunity for competitors to develop skills and make industry connections from home.

“You don’t have to move down [to Charlotte],” Smith said when discussing the advantages sim racing offers aspiring racers and their families.

Even drivers already competing in real-world racing are embracing simulators as a valuable training tool. Smith pointed to NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series driver Caden Honeycutt and ARCA competitor Brenden “Butterbean” Queen as examples of racers using sim racing to supplement their on-track development.

As esports and traditional motorsports continue to intersect, Smith believes the simulator is no longer simply a game. It has become a legitimate entry point. And for many aspiring drivers, engineers, and creators, it may be the most accessible path available.

In other news, read about what the Project Motor Racing team is doing to regain players’ trust after a troubled launch. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: EA Sports F1 25: 2026 Season Pack Out Now—Full Details
item.content:encoded:
EA Sports has released its 2026 Season pack for F1 25, and, with it, has announced full details of what players will get when they pick it up on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, or PC.

The 2026 Season Pack costs $24.99 as a standalone purchase, but is included with an EA Play Pro membership.

First, the F1 25: 2026 Season Pack will feature the new Cadillac and Audi Revolut teams. In addition, drivers will be updated to reflect their current teams, including Valtteri Bottas and Sergio Perez driving for Cadillac, the first major American car manufacturer to join F1.

Aside from the teams and drivers, the Spanish Grand Prix also debuts in the game with the MADRING circuit in Madrid, Spain. One caveat with it, however, is that it’s only accessible with the 2026 cars due to it being designed for the new season.

Finally, the 2026 Season Pack lets players drive the next generation of F1, with smaller cars and active aerodynamics.

“This year, players have a bigger role to play than ever before in making even more tactical and strategic decisions throughout a race,” Codemasters says. “Choose when to deploy the new Overtake Mode for a jaw-dropping boost of power, perfect for strategic overtakes and intense battles. Plus, enjoy real-time adjustments to your front and rear wings with Active Aerodynamics, optimizing your speed in every corner and straight. For added immersion on the track, the 2026 Season Pack’s new assists can help manage everything behind the scenes.” 

Codemasters’ senior creative director Lee Mather says the season pack was designed to “truly embody a new era of F1”.

“We’re putting players in the driver’s seat to rewrite the grid their way, from strategically leveraging Overtake Mode and Active Aerodynamics to propel yourself to the finish line to becoming the first to put the pedal to the metal on the new MADRING Circuit, there are so many ways to make the competition your own this year,” he said.

The F1 25: 2026 Season Pack comes in place of a new release for this year. EA Sports announced in November that it wasn’t going to release a new game in 2026, instead focusing on a brand new experience for 2027.

Are you going to be picking up the F1 25: 2026 Season Pack? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out the official covers for College Football 27 ahead of its full reveal on June 4. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Nintendo Direct Coming Next Week, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has claimed that a new Nintendo Direct is planned for next week and will be a general one.

The last Nintendo Direct took place in February 2026, which was a Partner Showcase. Players have been eagerly anticipating a new general event to discover more about all the upcoming games for their latest console. There are several rumored titles that fans are excited to hear about, as well as announced games that need release dates. All of this information could be revealed very soon.

Nintendo Direct Will Arrive in the Second Week of June, and it’s a General Direct

In the latest episode of his podcast, reliable insider Nate the Hate mentioned that he has heard the Nintendo Direct is expected to take place in the second week of June. When asked whether this refers to the week starting on June 8, Nate confirmed, saying “Yes,” suggesting that a Direct announcement is imminent.

This Direct could feature a variety of first-party and third-party announcements. Many fans are particularly curious about the release date for Duskbloods and the rumored Ocarina of Time remake. However, it’s important to note that these announcements are still speculative.

There are a lot of hopes and expectations from fans towards the upcoming Nintendo Direct, as the Switch 2 just doesn’t have enough games yet. Hopefully, there are some big surprises in store for players at this event. The last general Nintendo Direct was in September 2025, and as Nate pointed out, this upcoming Direct will kick off the Switch 2’s second year.

In other news, ARC Raiders has appeared online, being played on a Nintendo console, but not the one you’re probably thinking of. Additionally, Activision has confirmed that Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is set to return to Nintendo consoles on the Switch 2. What are your thoughts on a Nintendo General Direct coming next week? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Game Pass Wave 1 additions for June 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Pokémon Champions Mobile and Mega Raichu Release Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo and the Pokémon Company have confirmed the mobile release of Pokémon Champions as well as the addition of Mega Raichu X and Y to the game.

Pokémon Champions will release on Android and iOS on Wednesday, June 17, 2026.

And upon that release, all players will receive a free Raichu as well as the Raichunite X and Raichunite Y Mega Stones.

June 17 will therefore likely be the start of Regulation M-B, the game’s third ranked season, and the date when new Pokémon will be added to Pokémon Champions.

This should see the release of a whole new roster of playable Pokémon in the game and new items.

Pokémon Champions Mobile Release

Players are able to pre-register for Pokémon Champions on the App Store and Google Play to download the game as soon as it’s available.

Just like the Nintendo Switch version, the game will be free to download, though there are purchasable extras including premium battle passes.

The Pokémon Company has also confirmed that those who have been playing on the Switch will have their save data carry over to the mobile version by linking their Nintendo Account.

Mega Raichu X and Y Pokémon Champions Gift

As mentioned prior, Pokémon Champions players will receive Raichu’s Mega Stones as a free gift in their in-game mailbox.

The gift will be available to anyone who logs in between Wednesday, June 17, and Tuesday, September 1, 2026, at 6:49 p.m. PDT (September 2 1:49 a.m. UTC).

The Pokémon Company has also confirmed the abilities of Mega Raichu X and Y.

Mega Raichu X will have Electric Surge, setting up Electric Terrain upon entry to boost electric-type moves by 30%.

With 135 attack, Mega Raichu X’s Fake Out and Electric Terrain-boosted Volt Tackle will definitely hurt.

Meanwhile, Mega Raichu Y has been given the ability No Guard, which stops any of its moves from missing.

With 160 Special Attack, its 100% accurate Zap Cannon and Focus Blast will one-shot a lot of the in-game roster.

Both Raichus will likely be popular, which is bad news for all the Charizard and Areodactyls in the current meta.

However, it is very good news for ground types already in the game, like Mega Steelix, Mega Golurk, and Garchomp.

What do you make of the Mega Raichu abilities? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see five Pokémon that would shake up the Pokémon Champions meta with a new roster being added soon.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: XBOX Game Pass June 2026 Wave 1 Titles Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Just days before the upcoming XBOX Games Showcase, June’s Wave 1 of Game Pass titles have been revealed.

Xbox has an exciting week ahead, featuring the Games Showcase on June 7 and a Gears of War E-Day direct presentation. Just before this major showcase, players are being informed about the games that will be added to the subscription service this month, as well as those that will be removed in June.

XBOX Game Pass June Wave 1 Brings 8 Games to Try Out

While the selection of games to kick off June isn’t the most exciting, there are several titles that players may want to explore. Notable highlights include Solarpunk, Undisputed, Persona 5 Royal, and others. This month features 9 additions, including 3 games launching on Day One and one game preview. Check out the complete list of games coming to Game Pass in June 2026, Wave 1, along with their release dates, platforms, and subscription tiers.

  • Herdling (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 4: Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, and PC Game Pass
  • Total Chaos (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 4: Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, and PC Game Pass
  • Solarpunk (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 8: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass (Day One)
  • Undisputed (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 8: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • Persona 5 Royal (Cloud, Console, and PC) – June 9: Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • Beastro (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 11: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Frog Sqwad (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 11: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions (Game Preview) (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, and PC) – June 11: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass (Day One)
  • Junkster (Cloud, XBOX Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) – June 16: Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass (Day One)

Games Leaving Game Pass in June

Along with the new additions, players will also see 5 games leave in June. The last chance to try out these titles before deciding whether to buy them is June 15. Here’s the full list from the blog:

  • Jurassic World Evolution 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Lost in Random: The Eternal Die (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Scott Pilgrim vs. The World (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Warhammer 40K Space Marine: Master Crafted Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Shredder’s Revenge (Cloud, Console, and PC)

In other news, a host of upcoming games have been added to XBOX Game Pass Coming Soon, suggesting they might appear at the upcoming showcase. Additionally, XBOX CEO Asha Sharma has revealed that the Game Pass price drop has benefited the company. What are your thoughts on the XBOX Game Pass additions for June 2026 Wave 1? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about ARC Raiders finally winning a Game of the Year award. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
